note_data.title: Reactivity of metals
keyPoints: Filtration is used for separating insoluble solid from liquid - liquid particles are small enough to go through filter paper pores while solid particles are too large
Crystallisation is used for obtaining solid from solid and liquid solution - involves heating solution to evaporate solvent, producing hot saturated solution, then crystals form on cooling
Distillation is used for obtaining pure liquid from solution - pure liquid has low BP and changes to gas easily while solid has high BP and remains in flask
Fractional distillation separates 2 miscible liquids - liquid with lowest BP comes out first at top of fractionating column
In filtration, the solid obtained is called 'Residue' and the liquid obtained is called 'Filtrate'
In distillation and fractional distillation, the liquid obtained is called 'Distillate' and is collected in conical flask
A saturated solution means no more solid can dissolve in it
The constant temperature during distillation process equals the boiling point of the pure liquid
Chromatography is used for separating mixture of organic compounds such as colour dyes

definitions.definition: no more solid can dissolve in it

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
State what will happen and give a reason based on reactivity

modelAnswer.question: Zinc powder is added to copper sulfate solution. State what you would observe and explain why this reaction occurs.
modelAnswer.answer: The blue color of copper sulfate solution disappears and a brown/reddish solid forms. This reaction occurs because zinc is more reactive than copper, so zinc displaces copper from copper sulfate solution.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Zinc is more reactive than copper / displacement occurs

commonMistakes: Confusing which separation method to use - remember filtration is for insoluble solid and liquid, crystallisation for obtaining solid from solution
Not understanding that crystallisation involves formation of crystals from cooling liquid or saturated solution
Mixing up the terms residue (solid from filtration) and distillate (liquid from distillation)
examinerTips: Any question asking for method to obtain crystals, the process will be crystallisation
Remember that distillation works because pure liquid has low BP while solid has high BP
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Reactivity series arranged vertically with most reactive (potassium) at top and least reactive (gold) at bottom, with hydrogen positioned between lead and copper
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q3 (4 marks) — they asked students to explain how the reactivity of metals can be demonstrated using a reactivity series
N/D 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) — tested the order of reactivity of metals W, X, Y and Z based on their reactions with water and dilute sulfuric acid
M/J 2024 P2 Q2(c) (3 marks) — students had to explain how displacement reactions demonstrate relative metal reactivity
N/D 2023 P2 Q4(a) (5 marks) — required prediction of displacement reactions between different metals and metal salt solutions
M/J 2022 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — tested which metal would displace copper from copper sulfate solution

workedExample.question: Zinc powder is added to copper sulfate solution. A reaction occurs. When copper powder is added to zinc sulfate solution, no reaction occurs. Explain what these observations tell us about the reactivity of zinc and copper. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify that zinc displaces copper from copper sulfate solution, showing zinc is more reactive than copper
Step 2: Write the displacement reaction equation: Zn + CuSO₄ → ZnSO₄ + Cu
Step 3: Explain that copper cannot displace zinc from zinc sulfate because copper is less reactive than zinc
Step 4: State the general rule: more reactive metals displace less reactive metals from their compounds
workedExample.answer: Zinc displaces copper from copper sulfate solution (Zn + CuSO₄ → ZnSO₄ + Cu), showing zinc is more reactive than copper. Copper cannot displace zinc from zinc sulfate solution because copper is less reactive than zinc. This demonstrates that more reactive metals can displace less reactive metals from their compounds, which is the basis of the reactivity series.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for identifying zinc is more reactive than copper, 1 mark for correct equation, 1 mark for explaining why copper doesn't displace zinc, 1 mark for stating the general displacement rule

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to write balanced equations for displacement reactions' — always include the complete balanced chemical equation when describing displacement reactions
Examiner Report 2024: 'Students often confused the direction of displacement reactions' — remember that only MORE reactive metals can displace LESS reactive metals from their compounds

Examiner Report 2022: 'Common error was stating metals react with water without specifying cold water, steam, or no reaction' — always specify the conditions (cold water, steam, or dilute acid) when describing metal reactivity
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw the reactivity series as a vertical list with potassium at the top (most reactive) descending through sodium, calcium, magnesium, aluminum, zinc, iron, lead, copper, silver, and gold at the bottom (least reactive). Include horizontal lines to show: metals above aluminum react with cold water, metals from aluminum to lead react with steam, metals from zinc to lead react with dilute acids, and metals below lead (copper, silver, gold) do not react with dilute acids.
note_data.quickRecap: Separation methods depend on the type of mixture: filtration for insoluble solid and liquid, crystallisation to obtain solid from solution, distillation to obtain pure liquid from solution, and fractional distillation for miscible liquids. Each method produces specific products with distinct names like residue, filtrate, and distillate.
